_provenance: Claude-authored original. DeepSeek not used. No official answer; rubric-scored estimate only.

tasks.prompt: Write an essay of 150-200 words on the topic: Should artificial intelligence be allowed to make decisions that affect human lives, such as in medicine or criminal justice? Develop a clear argument: acknowledge the efficiency and consistency AI can bring, then weigh these against concerns about accountability, bias, and the loss of human judgement, and reach a reasoned position. Argument requirements: at least one concession and one rebuttal; cause-and-effect reasoning linking automation to social consequences. Structure: an introduction with a clear thesis, two developed body paragraphs, and a conclusion that restates your stance. Audience: readers of a university opinion column who expect nuanced argument rather than slogans.
tasks.promptZh: 就「是否应允许人工智能在医疗、司法等关乎人命的领域做决策」写一篇 150-200 词议论文,权衡效率与问责、偏见、人类判断的丧失,并给出有理有据的立场。
referencePoints: argument requirements: a clear thesis, at least one concession + rebuttal, cause-effect reasoning on automation and accountability
structure hints: intro with thesis + two developed body paragraphs + conclusion restating stance
scoring focus: depth of argument on ethics and responsibility, advanced vocabulary, varied syntax, coherence and cohesion
reference points guide scoring only; they are not a model essay

tasks.prompt: Write an essay of 150-200 words on the topic: As cities grow ever larger, does urbanization improve quality of life or erode it? Develop a balanced argument: recognize the economic opportunities and services that cities concentrate, then weigh them against congestion, pollution, and the weakening of community ties, and arrive at a defensible conclusion. Argument requirements: at least one concession and one rebuttal; a clear cause-and-effect chain explaining how rapid growth produces social strain. Structure: introduction with a thesis, two body paragraphs each developing one side, and a conclusion. Audience: a panel reviewing urban-policy essays who value evidence-based reasoning over emotional appeals.
tasks.promptZh: 就「城市化究竟提升还是侵蚀生活质量」写一篇 150-200 词议论文,权衡机遇与拥堵、污染、社区纽带弱化,得出可辩护的结论。
referencePoints: argument requirements: thesis on net effect of urbanization, concession + rebuttal, cause-effect chain on rapid growth and social strain
structure hints: intro + two body paragraphs (one per side) + conclusion
scoring focus: balanced reasoning, precise lexis for social and economic concepts, cohesive devices
reference points guide scoring only; they are not a model essay

tasks.prompt: Write an essay of 150-200 words on the topic: Does equal access to quality education remain a realistic goal, or is educational inequality widening? Build an argument that examines the structural causes of unequal opportunity, considers whether technology and reform can close the gap, and reaches a position on what should take priority. Argument requirements: at least one concession and one rebuttal; cause-and-effect reasoning linking family background to outcomes. Structure: an introduction stating your thesis, two body paragraphs analysing causes and possible remedies, and a conclusion. Audience: an education-policy forum that rewards analytical depth and discourages unsupported generalisations.
tasks.promptZh: 就「优质教育的平等机会是否仍现实,抑或教育不平等正在扩大」写一篇 150-200 词议论文,分析结构性成因并探讨技术与改革能否缩小差距。
referencePoints: argument requirements: thesis on educational equity, concession + rebuttal, cause-effect linking background to outcomes
structure hints: intro + two body paragraphs (causes / remedies) + conclusion
scoring focus: analytical depth on social structures, formal academic vocabulary, logical progression
reference points guide scoring only; they are not a model essay

tasks.prompt: Write an essay of 150-200 words on the topic: Should environmental protection take precedence over economic growth when the two conflict? Construct an argument that acknowledges the developmental needs of growing economies, then weighs them against the long-term costs of ecological damage, and defends a clear priority. Argument requirements: at least one concession and one rebuttal; cause-and-effect reasoning connecting present policy choices to future consequences. Structure: introduction with a thesis, two developed body paragraphs, and a conclusion proposing a balanced way forward. Audience: readers of an environmental-affairs magazine who expect arguments grounded in trade-offs rather than absolutes.
tasks.promptZh: 就「环境保护与经济增长冲突时是否应优先环保」写一篇 150-200 词议论文,权衡发展需求与生态长期代价,给出明确取舍。
referencePoints: argument requirements: thesis on environment-vs-growth priority, concession + rebuttal, cause-effect on policy and future cost
structure hints: intro + two body paragraphs + conclusion proposing balance
scoring focus: reasoning about trade-offs, advanced lexis for sustainability, coherent argumentation
reference points guide scoring only; they are not a model essay

tasks.prompt: Write an essay of 150-200 words on the topic: Has social media strengthened public debate or coarsened it? Develop an argument that recognizes how platforms broaden participation, then weighs this against echo chambers, misinformation, and declining civility, before reaching a reasoned judgement. Audience: a media-studies readership that expects you to engage with both the democratic promise and the documented harms of online communication.

tasks.prompt: Write an essay of 150-200 words on the topic: As remote work becomes common, does it liberate employees or blur the line between work and life? tasks.prompt: Write an essay of 150-200 words on the topic: Should governments fund the arts when resources are scarce? Develop an argument that acknowledges competing demands such as health and infrastructure, then defends or challenges the value of public support for culture, weighing tangible and intangible returns. Argument requirements: at least one concession and one rebuttal; cause-and-effect reasoning linking cultural investment to social benefits. Structure: an introduction with a thesis, two body paragraphs, and a conclusion that states your priority. Audience: a cultural-policy debate where readers reward you for engaging seriously with the opportunity cost of every funding decision.

tasks.prompt: Write an essay of 150-200 words on the topic: Does globalization benefit ordinary people or mainly serve large corporations? tasks.prompt: Write an essay of 150-200 words on the topic: Is automation a threat to employment or an engine of new opportunity?
